reasoning: The main obstacle preventing Polymarket US from launching is the U.S. government shutdown that began on October 1, 2025, which furloughed CFTC staff and stayed the self-certification of Polymarket's new contracts. However, Polymarket is technically and administratively ready to launch: they acquired a DCM (QCEX) for $112M, filed self-certifications on Sept 30 planning to list contracts on Oct 2, secured $2B in funding from ICE, and obtained a 'no-action' letter from the CFTC showing regulatory cooperation. A U.S. government shutdown has never historically lasted three full months, meaning it is highly likely to end before late December 2025. Once the shutdown ends and the CFTC returns to full operation, the stay on their contracts will be lifted, allowing them to go live almost immediately (typically just a 1-day wait period). Since the resolution only requires a single real-money trade, this will happen the day they go live. The prediction market placed the odds at 93% on October 16 despite the ongoing shutdown. Giving a slight penalty for the shutdown dragging into late October without immediate resolution, my final probability is 88%.
